title: Scout Microtransit Program Records, RFP 604918-25/PCD, Contracts, Planning Materials, and Ridership Reports (County Manager)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to Chapter 119, Florida Statutes, and Article I, Section 24 of the Florida Constitution, I request the following public records related to Seminole County’s Scout microtransit program and RFP 604918-25/PCD:

1. All vendor proposals submitted in response to RFP 604918-25/PCD, including proposals from BeFree LLC, Circuit Transit Inc., and River North Transit LLC, d/b/a Via Transportation Inc.

2. Evaluation committee score sheets, scoring records, ranking documentation, evaluator comments, tabulations, and any other records reflecting the evaluation and ranking of proposals submitted in response to RFP 604918-25/PCD.

3. A native-digital copy of Exhibit A-1 (Best and Final) from the executed agreement with BeFree LLC.

4. Planning documents, feasibility studies, analyses, memoranda, presentations, briefing materials, and similar records prepared by county staff during Summer and Fall 2024 concerning evaluation of microtransit alternatives, including any materials relating to the study visit to Volusia County referenced in the November 12, 2024 Board of County Commissioners work session presentation.

5. The Title VI fare structure compliance analysis prepared by any outside consultant and referenced at the May 20, 2025 Board of County Commissioners meeting.

6. The official notification letter or notice sent to LYNX following the May 20, 2025 Board of County Commissioners vote awarding the Scout microtransit contract.

7. Performance data and monthly ridership reports for the Scout program from October 1, 2025 through the most recent available reporting period.

If any portion of the requested records exists in electronic form, I request production in electronic format. If any responsive records are already publicly available online, please identify them with sufficient specificity to permit direct access.

If any part of this request is denied, please state in writing the specific legal basis for each withholding, including the applicable statutory exemption.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Drake Thomsen

title: OVP ARMS Search Results (Feb 2006) and Aragoncillo WAVES Request (Aug 2001)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All records returned by a search of the Office of the Vice President (OVP) Automated Records Management System (ARMS) conducted on or about February 27, 2006, the results of which were stored under the path "Search_02_27_2006_OVP > ARMS > RESULTS." I am aware of this search based on a record that surfaced in the Barack Obama Presidential Library's Office of Administration collection under FOIA case 22-18632-F. That record — document ID 1695_p_ru3a3004_vpo.txt, dated August 14, 2001 — is an Official USSS WAVES Request created by Leandro A. Aragoncillo of the Office of the Vice President for a visitor named Jeffrey Epstein to meet with Bruce Miller at OEOB Room 290 on August 15, 2001. I am requesting the complete set of results from this February 2006 ARMS search, not only the single record that appeared in the Obama Library's OA collection.

2. Any records from the Office of the Vice President identifying the role, title, or office held by Bruce Miller in August 2001, including staff directories, organizational charts, or personnel records. Bruce Miller later served as Assistant to the Vice President for Homeland Security Affairs (per the 2007-2008 U.S. Government Manual, page 90), but his position in August 2001 is not reflected in publicly available Congressional Directories from that period.

Background
Leandro Aragoncillo was a Marine military aide assigned to the Office of the Vice President from 1999 to 2001, serving under both Vice Presidents Gore and Cheney. He was arrested in September 2005 and convicted in 2007 of espionage. The February 2006 date of the ARMS search falls within the post-arrest investigation period. I have separately filed a broader request with this library for electronic records mentioning Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell across WAVES, ARMS, and Exchange systems; this supplemental request targets the specific 2006 search results and Bruce Miller identification, which would not necessarily be returned by a name-based keyword search.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: Public Records Request – Records of Forwarding from UC Office of the President to UC Berkeley (Sept 2025 Submission)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Public Records Request – Records of Forwarding from UC Office of the President to UC Berkeley (Sept 2025 Submission)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code §6250 et seq.), I hereby request records sufficient to show the receipt, forwarding, transmission, and administrative processing of materials submitted to the University of California, Office of the President by Young Jin Jung in September 2025.

Two certified mail deliveries were sent to the Office of the President:

Tracking number: 9468211105495866736472
(Government Claim form)
Tracking number: 9468211105495863220776
(Letter-form narrative containing the detailed factual description, damages, and supporting information referenced in the claim)

The Government Claim form referenced the factual description and damages using “see attached,” while the full narrative and supporting details were transmitted in the accompanying letter delivery.

It is my understanding that these materials were forwarded to the University of California, Berkeley. Accordingly, I request records sufficient to show the following:

The date the submission associated with tracking number 9468211105495866736472 was received by the Office of the President.
Whether the materials associated with both certified mail deliveries were forwarded to the University of California, Berkeley, and if so, the date of such forwarding.
Any transmittal records, routing records, internal logs, or correspondence reflecting the forwarding of these materials to the University of California, Berkeley.
The identity of the office, department, or individual at the University of California, Berkeley to whom the materials were forwarded.
Any claim file number, claim reference number, or administrative identifier assigned prior to or after forwarding.
Any notice of rejection issued regarding the submission, including the date such notice was mailed or transmitted.
Records sufficient to show whether the submission was accepted, rejected, deemed insufficient, or otherwise acted upon as a Government Claim, including any records reflecting a determination regarding its validity or sufficiency.

This request seeks only administrative records reflecting receipt, logging, routing, forwarding, and claim-processing status. It does not request privileged communications, investigative materials, or attorney work product.

If any responsive records are withheld, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions as required under the California Public Records Act.

If the University contends that no responsive records exist, please confirm whether any record exists reflecting receipt, logging, routing, forwarding, or any determination regarding the above certified mail tracking numbers.

If the records exist in electronic form, I request that they be provided electronically.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Epstein WAVES Records from FOIA 22-18632-F and Aug 12 2014 Screening`
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Cain,

Thank you for the prompt response. I have downloaded and reviewed all seven ZIP archives from the 22-18632-F finding aid, including the four partially-released WAVES records and all manifest files. I do not contest the WAVES record redactions.

However, my original request contained three items, and items 2 and 3 were not addressed by the existing release:

Item 2 requested all visitor clearance requests or Secret Service screening submissions for Jeffrey Epstein or Ghislaine Maxwell submitted by any White House office during 2009-2017. The WAVES records in 22-18632-F are completed visit entries. Screening submissions — including requests that were denied, withdrawn, or pending — are generated upstream of the WAVES system and are a distinct record category not covered by the existing release.

Item 3 requested visitor clearance requests submitted specifically by Kathryn Ruemmler (White House Counsel, June 2011 - May 2014) for Jeffrey Epstein, Ghislaine Maxwell, or known aliases. This is a search by submitter name that was not part of 22-18632-F.

Additionally, the manifest files in the WHO (White House Office) collection reveal 77 records withheld in full. The file paths in the manifests indicate many of these are White House Counsel's Office review and processing files for the WAVES records (stored under paths including "White House Counsel > WAVES" and "OUTGOING COUNSEL MEMOS > Political Guidance > WAVES"). These internal review files are responsive to my request regarding visitor clearance decisions and were not addressed in the 22-18632-F release.

I respectfully ask that this request be reopened to address items 2 and 3, and to process the 77 withheld WHO records for possible release.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

title: Liquid Funding Ltd in Maiden Lane LLC / Bear Stearns Bailout Records

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Liquid Funding Ltd in Maiden Lane LLC / Bear Stearns Bailout Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to Liquid Funding, Ltd., a Bermuda-incorporated structured finance entity (IBC RUCEC 29378), including but not limited to:

1. Any records related to Liquid Funding, Ltd. in connection with the Maiden Lane LLC portfolio created in March 2008 to facilitate JPMorgan Chase's acquisition of Bear Stearns.
2. Any records identifying whether Liquid Funding's assets, liabilities, or commercial paper were among those transferred to, purchased by, or guaranteed through Maiden Lane LLC or any other Federal Reserve emergency lending facility (2007-2009).
3. Correspondence, memoranda, or analyses referencing Liquid Funding, Ltd., Liquid Funding Holdings LLC, or Global Securitization Services LLC in connection with Bear Stearns' collapse or the Maiden Lane transaction.
4. Any records of due diligence, governance review, or officer/director background checks conducted on Liquid Funding, Ltd. or its parent entities in connection with the Maiden Lane facility.

To the extent responsive records are held primarily by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, I request any Board of Governors records reflecting oversight, approval, analysis, or review of such records or of the Maiden Lane facility's asset composition.

Background: Liquid Funding, Ltd. had $6.7 billion in outstanding liabilities (Fitch, 2006) and was 40% owned by Bear Stearns. Its commercial paper was held in retail money market funds. Bear Stearns Bank Plc (Dublin) managed its investments. JPMorgan Chase served as Security Trustee. The entity's rated liabilities were reportedly paid in full on April 18, 2008, approximately one month after the Fed-facilitated Bear Stearns bailout. Jeffrey Epstein served as Director and Chairman of this entity from November 2001 to March 2007. I am seeking to determine whether Federal Reserve emergency facilities backstopped this entity's obligations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request – Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-210 – Incident Report – Jose Morales / Christine Holloway – December 2–3, 2019 – 6½ Myrtle Avenue, Ansonia, CT
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the incident/offense report for the homicide investigation of Christine Holloway, initiated on December 2–3, 2019, at 6½ Myrtle Avenue, Ansonia, CT.

Victim: Christine Holloway (age 43)
Suspect: Jose Morales (age 48, of New Haven, CT)
Incident: On December 2, 2019, the Ansonia Police Department conducted welfare checks at 6½ Myrtle Avenue after Holloway failed to report to work. Officers forced entry at approximately 7:30 PM and discovered Holloway deceased in the bathtub, having died of blunt force trauma. Holloway's 14-month-old daughter, Vanessa Morales, was not found at the scene. Morales was initially arrested on December 3, 2019, on weapons charges. He was subsequently charged with murder and tampering with evidence on February 7, 2020.
Case status: Fully adjudicated. Morales was convicted of murder and tampering with evidence on April 22, 2025, following a jury trial at Milford Superior Court, and sentenced to 65 years in prison on July 14, 2025.

Please include all narrative portions, supplemental reports, and officer observations associated with this incident covering the period of December 2–3, 2019. I request electronic delivery where possible. Please provide a written fee estimate before processing. If any portion is denied, please identify the specific exemption under the Connecticut FOIA authorizing the withholding and release all segregable non-exempt records.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: SLP PD Flock Safety Audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) ALPR Audit Trail Logs (All Access & Activity)
Records comprising the ALPR data audit trail documenting all queries, searches, alerts, access, sharing, or dissemination events from 1/1/2025 through the date this request is processed. Per department policy, all such actions must be recorded in a data audit trail. Please include all available metadata, including but not limited to:
- Date and time
- User identifier (name or role)
- Query/search parameters
- Action taken (search, alert review, data access, sharing, etc.)

Please provide these records in a structured format (e.g., CSV, XLSX, or similar database format).
------
2) Access Justification / Purpose Logs
Records documenting the purpose, justification, or case association for each ALPR query or access event during the same time period. Department policy restricts access to legitimate law enforcement purposes tied to investigations or official actions. 
------
3) External Access / Inter-Agency Query Logs
Records of any access to SLP PD ALPR data by external agencies, including:
- Requesting agency
- Requestor identity (or role)
- Date/time
- Stated purpose
- Associated case number (if available)

Department policy requires written requests and documented justification for such access.
[Note - Common Flock usage logs may group 1, 2, and 3 together into one excel file. That is hopefully less work for you, and easier for us.] 
------
4) Manual Hot List Entries and Justifications
Records of all Manual Hot List entries during the requested time period, including:
- Plate entered
- Date of entry/removal
- Justification or associated investigation

Policy requires a “legitimate and specific law enforcement reason” for such entries.
------
5) Agreements with Private Entities (Flock / ALPR Access)
Any agreements, memoranda, or contracts with:

- Homeowner associations
- Private businesses
- Individuals
- Religious entities or groups

Relating to ALPR or Flock system deployment, access, or data sharing.
------
6) Electronic Communications with Flock Safety
Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SLP PD personnel and Flock Safety (domain: @flocksafety.com). Timeframe: 6/1/2025 to present
You may de-duplicate mass emails or newsletters.
------
7) Biennial ALPR Audit Reports and Submissions
Copies of all biennial ALPR audit reports, including any reports, summaries, or submissions provided to the Minnesota Commissioner of Administration, legislative committees, or other oversight bodies, for the past two years.
------

I realize this is a large request and appreciate you taking the time to complete it. Please feel free to respond in multiple parts as documents become available.

I have already reviewed: 
A)  Flock Transparency Page: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/saint-louis-park-mn-pd
B) SLP PD ALPR Policy: https://www.stlouisparkmn.gov/home/showpublisheddocument/28047/638681458304130000
C) Location of Devices: https://dps.mn.gov/divisions/bca/data-and-reports/agencies-use-lprs-lpr

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Robert Mueller

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Robert Mueller
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into former FBI Director Robert Swan Mueller III (August 7, 1944 – March 20, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/21/us/politics/robert-s-mueller-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti
